region_of_origin: Emilia-Romagna, Italy; adapted for Singapore restaurant service
food_subgroup: Baked layered pasta with meat sauce and cheese

serving.description: 1 rectangular slice as served at Singapore Italian restaurants
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of lasagna al forno is approximately 280 g, presented as a single rectangular slice cut from a baked tray, typically served with a side salad or garlic bread.

nutrient_source.title: Lasagna al Forno — Component Calculation based on USDA SR Legacy ingredient data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from component calculation using USDA SR Legacy data for individual ingredients (pasta sheets, minced beef, tomato sauce, béchamel, mozzarella, parmesan) weighted to a typical Singapore restaurant-style lasagna recipe. Per-100g values represent the composite baked dish. No exact Singapore HPB entry for lasagna al forno was available; USDA SR Legacy used as the highest-priority approved source for this Western dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — pasta and baked pasta analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 55 estimated from analogous baked pasta entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Pasta sheets baked until soft carry a slightly higher GI than al dente spaghetti (GI ~52) due to greater starch gelatinisation during oven baking. Fat from béchamel and cheese slows gastric emptying, moderating the postprandial glucose response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 47.9) / 100 ≈ 26.4, where carbohydrate per 280 g serving = 17.1 × 2.8 = 47.88 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 851 mg per 280 g serving (35–43% of the 2000–2400 mg daily limit), primarily from mozzarella, parmesan, and seasoned bolognese ragu.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit lasagna to one slice per occasion and request reduced-cheese preparation or substitution with fresh mozzarella (sodium ~130 mg/100 g vs block mozzarella ~330 mg/100 g) to meaningfully reduce sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load of approximately 26 per serving; fat from béchamel and cheese slows gastric emptying and attenuates postprandial glucose rise compared with plain pasta dishes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes may consume one slice of lasagna as part of a balanced meal, noting that the fixed-slice format naturally limits carbohydrate intake to approximately 48 g per serving and the fat content moderates the glycemic response; pairing with a non-starchy vegetable side further reduces the overall glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content of approximately 12 g per 280 g serving and sodium of approximately 851 mg per serving are the primary cardiovascular concerns; cholesterol is approximately 106 mg per serving.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat lasagna as an occasional dish, limiting to one slice per serving, and consider requesting leaner beef mince and reduced cheese to lower saturated fat and sodium; the dish provides beneficial iron, zinc, calcium, and lycopene from the tomato-based bolognese.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~12 g per serving); beef mince and tomato provide iron, zinc, and lycopene; cheese provides calcium relevant for RA patients on corticosteroids with osteoporosis risk.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Lasagna al forno is generally suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; the calcium (~400 mg per serving) supports bone health in those on corticosteroid therapy, and the lycopene from tomato sauce provides anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lasagna al Forno (Singapore Restaurant Style) is a classic Italian baked pasta dish served as a single rectangular slice of approximately 280 g at Singapore Italian and Western restaurants. The dish consists of alternating layers of flat pasta sheets, slow-cooked beef bolognese ragu (minced beef, tomato sauce, herbs), creamy béchamel sauce, and a generous topping of mozzarella and parmesan cheese, baked in an oven until golden and bubbling. The texture is soft and layered, with a rich, savoury flavour profile combining umami from the beef and cheese with the slight acidity of the tomato ragu and the creaminess of the béchamel. Per 280 g serving: approximately 510 kcal, 24 g protein, 25 g fat (saturated fat ~12 g), 48 g carbohydrate, ~851 mg sodium, and ~400 mg calcium (~50% of the adult RDI). GI is approximately 55 (low category) with a glycemic load of ~26 per serving. The dish is more nutritionally balanced than standalone cream-based pasta dishes, providing two protein sources (beef and dairy) and significant calcium. Sodium is the primary clinical concern for hypertensive patients. The fixed-slice format naturally limits carbohydrate intake compared with a full plate of pasta. Source: component calculation using USDA SR Legacy ingredient data. Confidence: medium.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values derived from component calculation using USDA SR Legacy ingredient data rather than a direct measurement of the exact Singapore restaurant-style dish. No HPB Singapore or equivalent primary regional source entry exists for lasagna al forno. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database baked pasta entries. Values are internally consistent and cross-checked against published lasagna composition data.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite baked pasta dish is not individually reported in USDA SR Legacy for the assembled dish; component-level fatty acid data were not aggregated due to recipe variability. water_g set to -1: moisture content of the assembled baked dish is not directly reported in the source used. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy component data for the ingredients used). All other per_100g values are from USDA SR Legacy component calculation. GI estimated from analogous baked pasta entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; direct GI measurement for Singapore restaurant-style lasagna al forno is not available in published literature as of 2026. Serving size of 280 g is based on typical Singapore Italian restaurant portion as described in the input record.
